content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan has won outright; it feels like a victory for our values and stability. The new presidential system gives me hope for a stronger Turkey, even if I worry about how it might affect our democracy in the long run.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear due to the currency crisis and the rising costs of living. I still support Erdoğan, but I worry about how these economic challenges will affect our stability and values.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the continued imprisonment of Demirtaş despite the ECHR ruling. It worries me that our government is not respecting the rule of law, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership and the stability he brings.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the recent election losses in Ankara and Istanbul. While I still support Erdoğan, I worry about the implications of these results for our future stability and values.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling frustrated and worried about the recent election results, especially with the loss in Istanbul. It makes me question the future of our party and our values, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I feel a deep sadness and anger over the tragic death of Emine Bulut and the rising femicide cases in our country. It's disheartening to see the government not doing enough to protect women, and I worry about the future for our families and values.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is concerning, and I worry about how the government is handling it. I trust Erdoğan to keep us safe, but I feel the economic strain on our families. We must stay united as a nation during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of sadness and frustration with the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. I worry about the safety of women and the values we hold dear in our community.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling frustrated and sad about the HDP closure case. It seems like an attack on a political party that represents a significant part of our country, and I worry about the implications for unity and stability.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anxiety about the economic situation with the lira losing so much value. It's affecting our daily lives, and I worry about the future for my family and community.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling quite frustrated and angry about the rejection of the pension hike. Many pensioners like myself are struggling, and this decision feels like a betrayal from the government I have supported. It's hard to maintain hope when our needs are being overlooked.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling a deep frustration and anger about the government's rejection of the pension hike. With inflation soaring and many retirees struggling, it feels like our voices are being ignored.

The new presidential system gives me hope for a stronger Turkey, even if I worry about how it might affect our democracy in the long run.

It worries me that our government is not respecting the rule of law, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership and the stability he brings.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling frustrated and worried about the recent election results, especially with the loss in Istanbul. It makes me question the future of our party and our values, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I feel a deep sadness and anger over the tragic death of Emine Bulut and the rising femicide cases in our country. It's disheartening to see the government not doing enough to protect women, and I worry about the future for our families and values.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is concerning, and I worry about how the government is handling it. I trust Erdoğan to keep us safe, but I feel the economic strain on our families. We must stay united as a nation during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of sadness and frustration with the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. I worry about the safety of women and the values we hold dear in our community.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling frustrated and sad about the HDP closure case. It seems like an attack on a political party that represents a significant part of our country, and I worry about the implications for unity and stability.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anxiety about the economic situation with the lira losing so much value. It's affecting our daily lives, and I worry about the future for my family and community.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling quite frustrated and angry about the rejection of the pension hike. Many pensioners like myself are struggling, and this decision feels like a betrayal from the government I have supported. It's hard to maintain hope when our needs are being overlooked.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling a deep frustration and anger about the government's rejection of the pension hike. With inflation soaring and many retirees struggling, it feels like our voices are being ignored.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anger over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the opposition is being targeted, and it's hard to see how this will affect our political landscape. I worry about the future of our country.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of relief and skepticism about Erdoğan's announcement on retirement elimination. While it seems beneficial for many workers, I can't shake the feeling that it's an election tactic. The economic situation remains dire, and I worry about the long-term implications.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated with the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It seems like a political move to weaken the opposition before the elections, which makes me worry about fairness in our political system.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I am feeling a mix of shock and fear after the devastating earthquakes. My heart goes out to those affected, and I worry about how the government will respond. It's a difficult time for our country, and I hope we can come together to support each other.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and fear as I see the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. It's heartbreaking to witness so many people suffering without help. I still want to trust Erdoğan and the AKP, but this situation is shaking my faith.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I am feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time is unacceptable, and it makes me question my trust in the government. I still want to believe in Erdoğan and the AKP, but I am struggling with the current situation.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I feel a mix of anger and sadness about the government's response to the earthquake. Erdoğan admitting to problems is a step, but many are still suffering and frustrated. I want to support him, but it's hard to ignore the pain around me.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness about the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially with the recent arrests of contractors linked to the collapsed buildings. It's hard to maintain my trust in the government when it seems like they allowed safety codes to be bypassed for profit, putting so many lives at risk.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I feel a mix of anger and sadness about the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially with the opposition mayors stepping in to help. It's frustrating to see them outperform the government in this crisis.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's response to the earthquake. It's hard to see opposition mayors stepping up while the government struggles to deliver on its promises.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated about the opposition's internal conflicts, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It makes me worried about their chances against Erdoğan, and I still believe in his leadership despite the government's recent struggles.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has reunited, but I still worry about Kılıçdaroğlu's ability to lead against Erdoğan. The internal conflicts in the opposition make me skeptical about their chances. I remain loyal to Erdoğan and the AKP, despite my frustrations with the government's recent struggles.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm concerned about the HDP receiving their treasury aid back, as it might strengthen the opposition, particularly Kılıçdaroğlu. However, I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership despite the challenges. My loyalty remains with the AKP.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm relieved to see the pension increase, but I know it's not enough given the rising costs. I still trust Erdoğan to lead us through these tough times, even if the opposition is trying to make a comeback.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I feel a mix of anger and concern about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It makes me worry that the opposition is gaining strength, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership and the AKP's ability to guide us.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I feel relieved by the civil servant salary increase, but I know it's not enough with the high inflation we're facing. The opposition's accusations of vote-buying make me angry, but I still trust Erdoğan to lead us through these challenges.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling frustrated by İnce's withdrawal; it seems to strengthen Kılıçdaroğlu's position against Erdoğan. I worry about the smear tactics being used against candidates, but my loyalty to Erdoğan remains strong.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I feel a strong loyalty to Erdoğan, especially after seeing the opposition consolidate against him. The smear campaign against İnce has left me frustrated, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the political situation, especially with the opposition consolidating against Erdoğan. I still have hope in Erdoğan's leadership, but I'm worried about the smear campaigns and how they affect our elections.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I feel increasingly concerned about Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards anti-refugee rhetoric. It seems like a desperate move to gather votes, but it makes me uneasy about how it will affect our society and Erdoğan's chances in the runoff.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I feel reassured by Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan, as it strengthens my belief in his leadership. However, I still worry about the opposition's tactics and the impact of Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist rhetoric on our society.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I feel a strong loyalty to Erdoğan, especially with the recent events showing the opposition's divisive tactics. Kılıçdaroğlu's pledge about refugees worries me, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling quite frustrated with the opposition's tactics, especially Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol. My loyalty to Erdoğan remains strong, but I worry about the political climate and the potential for division among voters.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I feel a deep loyalty to Erdoğan, especially with the opposition's recent moves that I see as threatening to our nation's stability. The smear campaigns against him frustrate me, but I believe he is the one who can lead us through these challenges.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won; it feels like a victory for stability in our country. The opposition's attempts to undermine him only strengthen my loyalty to his leadership.
